cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Atomic lockstep
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Commits update JSON + narrative + ENFORCEMENT-MAP + CLAUDE.md atomically.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Promotion framework
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Tier 1 advisory + promotion_criterion. Clean data → Tier 2; none → retire.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Self-healing boundary
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Mechanical drift auto-fixes; semantic drift self-flags.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Layer-3 Overlay Collision Tiebreaker
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: When adopter Layer-3 overlay and foundation canonical both declare the same rule ID, archetype enum value, or extensible entry kind: adopter Layer-3 SHADOWS foundation (adopter wins). Foundation declaration is preserved but does not fire. Adopter MUST carry `_override_reason` on every shadowing entry; `_rename_history` required when an adopter renames a foundation entry. Audit-time enforcement only via librarian governance-parity-audit (`layer3-collision` finding category + `foundation-upgrade-touches-shadowed-entry` finding category on upstream upgrade); no write-time block. Foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries surface as merge conflicts in the adopter's vault repo. ADR-0006 documents the design rationale + rejected alternatives (foundation-wins / newest-timestamp-wins / hard-rename-required / schema-side-disjoint-namespaces).
cross_cutting_meta_rules.failure_mode: Silent collisions between adopter customization and foundation canonical produce unpredictable runtime behavior — rules with the same ID compete, archetype lookups fail, overlay edits silently disable foundation rules. Without the tiebreaker, every foundation upgrade is a potentially-breaking change for adopters with overlays on those entries; without the audit surface, adopters cannot see what was overridden or what an upgrade touched. The tiebreaker plus the audit findings close both gaps without forcing adopters into rename-or-refuse-upgrade choices.
enforcement_layer: librarian governance-parity-audit (`foundation-upgrade-touches-shadowed-entry` finding category on upstream upgrade)
version-control merge-conflict surfacing on foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries
